snp2.7:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
snp2.7:1.3:
Then many Kosalan brahmans of great means—old, aged, advanced in years,
having come to the last stage of life—approached the Blessed One.
On arrival, they exchanged courteous greetings with him.
snp2.7:1.4:
After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, they sat to one side.
As they were sitting there, they said to him,
snp2.7:1.5: “Master Gotama, do brahmans at present live in conformity with the brahman principles of ancient brahmans?”
snp2.7:1.6:
“No, brahmans.
Brahmans at present don’t live in conformity with the brahman principles of ancient brahmans.”
snp2.7:1.7: “It would be good if Master Gotama described the brahman principles of ancient brahmans, if it wouldn’t burden him.”
snp2.7:1.8:
“In that case, brahmans, listen and pay close attention.
I will speak.”
snp2.7:1.9: “As you say, master,” the brahmans of great means responded to the Blessed One.
snp2.7:2.3: Abandoning the five strings of sensuality,
snp2.7:2.4: they practiced for their own benefit.
snp2.7:3.3: They had study as their wealth.
snp2.7:4.1: They did not despise what was prepared for them:
snp2.7:4.2: food set at doors, prepared out of conviction
snp2.7:5.4: honored brahmans with multi-colored clothes, bedding, & dwellings.
snp2.7:6.1: Brahmans protecting their principles, protected by law, were not to be violated, not to be beaten.
snp2.7:6.4: from the doors of any home.
snp2.7:7.3: Brahmans of old practiced the search
snp2.7:8.2: nor did they buy their wives.
snp2.7:8.4: having come together, they found joy together.
snp2.7:10.1:
They praised:
the holy life, virtue,
snp2.7:13.1: They asked for rice, bedding, cloth,
snp2.7:13.3: Having collected all that in line with rectitude,
snp2.7:13.4: from that they performed the sacrifice.
snp2.7:14.1: And in setting up the sacrifice,
snp2.7:14.3: “Like a mother, father, brother, or other relative,
snp2.7:16.3: brahmans were committed to standards of what should & shouldn’t be done
snp2.7:16.5: As long as this lasted in the world,
snp2.7:17.1: But a perversion came among them.
snp2.7:18.4: well-proportioned, planned & laid out lavish human wealth,
snp2.7:19.2: joined with groups of excellent women—
snp2.7:21.1: Then the king, lord of charioteers,
snp2.7:21.2: induced by the brahmans, having performed these sacrifices—
snp2.7:21.3: the horse sacrifice, the human sacrifice,
snp2.7:24.1: And they, receiving the wealth there,
snp2.7:24.2: found joy together in hoarding it.
snp2.7:24.6: they went up to Okkāka again.
snp2.7:25.4: This is a requisite for beings.
snp2.7:26.1: Then the king, lord of charioteers,
snp2.7:26.2: induced by the brahmans, killed in a sacrifice
snp2.7:26.3:
many hundred-thousands of cows.
The cows— meek like sheep, giving milk by the bucket— hadn’t,
snp2.7:27.1: with their hooves or horns or
snp2.7:27.2:
anything else, done anyone any harm.
But the king,
snp2.7:28.4: when the knife fell on the cows.
snp2.7:29.1: Three were the diseases before then:
snp2.7:30.4: the sacrificers fall away from the Dhamma.
snp2.7:31.3: Where people see such a thing,
snp2.7:32.1: With the Dhamma perishing in this way,
snp2.7:32.3: noble warriors are split far apart,
snp2.7:33.2: and any others protected by clan,
snp2.7:33.3: repudiating the doctrine of their birth, fall
snp2.7:34.1: When this was said, those brahmans of great means said to the Blessed One:
snp2.7:34.2:
“Magnificent, Master Gotama!
Magnificent!
Just as if he were to place upright what was overturned, to reveal what was hidden, to show the way to one who was lost,
or to carry a lamp into the dark so that those with eyes could see forms,
in the same way has Master Gotama—through many lines of reasoning—made the Dhamma clear.
snp2.7:34.3:
We go to Master Gotama for refuge, to the Dhamma, & to the Saṅgha of monks.
May Master Gotama remember us as lay followers who have gone for refuge from this day forward, for life.”
